extraction.one_line_summary: DEILLIER Silvan won from 190km breakaway, outsprinting STUYVEN Jasper who was tactically compromised by teammate PEDERSEN Mads' earlier work
tactical_patterns: PEDERSEN neutralized STUYVEN's sprint by doing excessive early work, allowing BMC's DEILLIER to conserve energy in 190km break.
Quick-Step controlled peloton with pink jersey despite having STUYVEN forward, revealing conservative race leadership priorities over stage hunting.
YOO Adam attempted late junction to breakaway with 5.5km remaining, suggesting weak GC team discipline when opportunity arose.

rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: negative: fastest on paper but lost sprint after teammate exhausted him marking moves, poor intra-break positioning management
rider_signals.DEILLIER Silvan: positive: won despite inferior sprint credentials by superior energy management in 200km effort, tactically waited perfectly
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: neutral: strong work rate but possibly compromised own team's stage chances by over-contributing before finale
rider_signals.POSTBERGER Lukas: neutral: initiated selection attacks with 5.5km to go but lacked finishing power to capitalize on aggression
